steps.title: Use Desmos to find the needed coefficient
steps.content: For an equation in $x$ to have no solutions, the $x$-coefficients must match but the constants must not match. In Desmos, enter $x_1=[1]$ and regress the $x$-terms: $9x_1\sim ax_1$. Desmos gives $a=9$, so statement I must be true.

steps.title: Find the excluded value of b
steps.content: With $a=9$, the equation becomes $9x+5=9x+9b$. If $5=9b$, the equation would have infinitely many solutions instead of no solutions. To find that excluded value in Desmos, enter $y_1=[5]$ and $y_1\sim9b$, which gives $b=\frac{5}{9}$. Therefore no solutions require $b\neq\frac{5}{9}$.

steps.title: Confirm algebraically
steps.content:
Expanding the right side gives $$9x+5=ax+ab.$$ Rearranging gives $$(9-a)x=ab-5.$$ No solution happens when the coefficient of $x$ is $0$ but the constant side is not $0$: $$9-a=0\Rightarrow a=9$$ and $$ab-5
eq0\Rightarrow9b
eq5\Rightarrow b
eq rac{5}{9}.$$

steps.title: Select the answer
steps.content: Statement I is required. Statement II is not required because $b$ can be any value except $\frac{5}{9}$. Statement III is required. Therefore only I and III must be true, which matches choice D.
